title: A Single-Fiber Optical Frequency Domain Reflectometry (OFDR)-Based Shape Sensing of Concentric Tube Steerable Drilling Robots
summary: This paper introduces a novel shape-sensing approach for Concentric Tube Steerable Drilling Robots (CT-SDRs) based on Optical Frequency Domain Reflectometry (OFDR). Unlike traditional FBG-based methods, OFDR enables continuous strain measurement along the entire fiber length with enhanced spatial resolution. In the proposed method, a Shape Sensing Assembly (SSA) is first fabricated by integrating a single OFDR fiber with a flat NiTi wire. The calibrated SSA is then routed through and housed within the internal channel of a flexible drilling instrument, which is guided by the pre-shaped NiTi tube of the CT-SDR. In this configuration, the drilling instrument serves as a protective sheath for the SSA during drilling, eliminating the need for integration or adhesion to the instrument surface that is typical of conventional optical sensor approaches. The performance of the proposed SSA, integrated within the cannulated CT-SDR, was thoroughly evaluated under free-bending conditions and during drilling along multiple J-shaped trajectories in synthetic Sawbones phantoms. Results demonstrate accurate and reliable shape-sensing capability, confirming the feasibility and robustness of this integration strategy.
tldr: This paper presents a novel shape-sensing method for Concentric Tube Steerable Drilling Robots using Optical Frequency Domain Reflectometry (OFDR), offering continuous strain measurement with high spatial resolution and eliminating the need for surface integration typical in conventional methods.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种基于光学频域反射仪（OFDR）的新型形状感知方法，用于同心管可操纵钻孔机器人，提供高空间分辨率的连续应变测量，并消除了传统方法中表面集成的需求。
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种基于光频域反射技术（OFDR）的同心管可转向钻孔机器人（CT-SDR）新型形状感知方法。与传统光纤光栅（FBG）技术不同，OFDR能够以更高空间分辨率实现沿光纤全长的连续应变测量。在该方法中，首先将单根OFDR光纤与扁平镍钛合金丝集成，制作出形状传感组件（SSA）。经标定后的SSA被穿入并固定于柔性钻孔器械的内腔通道中，该器械由CT-SDR的预成型镍钛管引导。这种构型使钻孔器械在作业过程中充当SSA的保护鞘，无需像传统光学传感器方案那样将传感元件集成或粘附于器械表面。研究通过自由弯曲实验及在合成人造骨模型中进行多组J形轨迹钻孔实验，全面评估了集成于中空CT-SDR内的SSA性能。结果表明该方法具备精确可靠的形状感知能力，验证了该集成策略的可行性与鲁棒性。

title: Consistency-Driven Dual LSTM Models for Kinematic Control of a Wearable Soft Robotic Arm
summary: In this paper, we introduce a consistency-driven dual LSTM framework for accurately learning both the forward and inverse kinematics of a pneumatically actuated soft robotic arm integrated into a wearable device. This approach effectively captures the nonlinear and hysteretic behaviors of soft pneumatic actuators while addressing the one-to-many mapping challenge between actuation inputs and end-effector positions. By incorporating a cycle consistency loss, we enhance physical realism and improve the stability of inverse predictions. Extensive experiments-including trajectory tracking, ablation studies, and wearable demonstrations-confirm the effectiveness of our method. Results indicate that the inclusion of the consistency loss significantly boosts prediction accuracy and promotes physical consistency over conventional approaches. Moreover, the wearable soft robotic arm demonstrates strong human-robot collaboration capabilities and adaptability in everyday tasks such as object handover, obstacle-aware pick-and-place, and drawer operation. This work underscores the promising potential of learning-based kinematic models for human-centric, wearable robotic systems.
tldr: The paper presents a dual LSTM framework for learning forward and inverse kinematics of a wearable soft robotic arm, addressing nonlinear behaviors and one-to-many mapping challenges with a cycle consistency loss, demonstrating effectiveness in human-robot collaboration tasks.
tldr_zh: 本文提出了一种双LSTM框架，用于学习可穿戴软体机械臂的正向和逆向运动学，通过循环一致性损失解决非线性行为和一对多映射问题，展示了在人机协作任务中的有效性。
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种基于一致性驱动的双长短期记忆网络框架，用于精确学习集成于可穿戴设备的气动驱动软体机械臂正向与逆向运动学。该方法有效捕捉了软体气动执行器的非线性与迟滞特性，同时解决了驱动输入与末端执行器位置之间一对多映射的难题。通过引入循环一致性损失函数，我们增强了物理真实性并提高了逆向预测的稳定性。大量实验——包括轨迹跟踪、消融研究和可穿戴演示——验证了本方法的有效性。结果表明，与传统方法相比，一致性损失的引入显著提升了预测精度并促进了物理一致性。此外，该可穿戴软体机械臂在物体传递、障碍物感知抓取放置及抽屉操作等日常任务中展现出优异的人机协作能力与适应性。本研究揭示了基于学习的运动学模型在以人为中心的可穿戴机器人系统中的广阔应用前景。

title: KineVLA: Towards Kinematics-Aware Vision-Language-Action Models with Bi-Level Action Decomposition
summary: In this paper, we introduce a novel kinematics-rich vision-language-action (VLA) task, in which language commands densely encode diverse kinematic attributes (such as direction, trajectory, orientation, and relative displacement) from initiation through completion, at key moments, unlike existing action instructions that capture kinematics only coarsely or partially, thereby supporting fine-grained and personalized manipulation. In this setting, where task goals remain invariant while execution trajectories must adapt to instruction-level kinematic specifications. To address this challenge, we propose KineVLA, a vision-language-action framework that explicitly decouples goal-level invariance from kinematics-level variability through a bi-level action representation and bi-level reasoning tokens to serve as explicit, supervised intermediate variables that align language and action. To support this task, we construct the kinematics-aware VLA datasets spanning both simulation and real-world robotic platforms, featuring instruction-level kinematic variations and bi-level annotations. Extensive experiments on LIBERO and a Realman-75 robot demonstrate that KineVLA consistently outperforms strong VLA baselines on kinematics-sensitive benchmarks, achieving more precise, controllable, and generalizable manipulation behaviors.
tldr: The paper introduces KineVLA, a kinematics-aware vision-language-action framework that decouples goal-level invariance from kinematics-level variability using bi-level action decomposition, enabling fine-grained and personalized robotic manipulation.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了KineVLA，一种基于双层次动作分解的运动感知视觉-语言-动作框架，将目标级不变性与运动级变异性解耦，实现细粒度和个性化的机器人操作。
summary_zh: 本文提出了一种新型运动学密集型视觉-语言-动作（VLA）任务，其语言指令从起始到完成阶段均密集编码了关键时刻的多样化运动学属性（如方向、轨迹、方位和相对位移），这与现有仅粗略或部分捕捉运动特性的动作指令形成鲜明对比，从而支持细粒度与个性化的操作控制。该任务场景下，任务目标保持恒定而执行轨迹需根据指令层级的运动学规范动态调整。为此，我们提出KineVLA框架，通过双层动作表征与双层推理标记，将目标层不变性与运动层变异性显式解耦，这些标记作为受监督的显式中间变量实现语言与动作的对齐。为支撑该任务，我们构建了覆盖仿真与真实机器人平台的运动感知VLA数据集，其特点是指令级运动学变体与双层标注。在LIBERO仿真环境和Realman-75实体机器人上的大量实验表明，KineVLA在运动学敏感基准测试中持续超越强VLA基线，实现了更精准、可控且可泛化的操作行为。

title: Real-Time Online Learning for Model Predictive Control using a Spatio-Temporal Gaussian Process Approximation
summary: Learning-based model predictive control (MPC) can enhance control performance by correcting for model inaccuracies, enabling more precise state trajectory predictions than traditional MPC. A common approach is to model unknown residual dynamics as a Gaussian process (GP), which leverages data and also provides an estimate of the associated uncertainty. However, the high computational cost of online learning poses a major challenge for real-time GP-MPC applications. This work presents an efficient implementation of an approximate spatio-temporal GP model, offering online learning at constant computational complexity. It is optimized for GP-MPC, where it enables improved control performance by learning more accurate system dynamics online in real-time, even for time-varying systems. The performance of the proposed method is demonstrated by simulations and hardware experiments in the exemplary application of autonomous miniature racing.
tldr: The paper presents an efficient spatio-temporal Gaussian Process (GP) approximation for real-time online learning in Model Predictive Control (MPC), enabling improved control performance with constant computational complexity, demonstrated in autonomous miniature racing.
tldr_zh: 该论文提出了一种高效的时空高斯过程（GP）近似方法，用于模型预测控制（MPC）中的实时在线学习，以恒定的计算复杂度实现更好的控制性能，并在自主微型赛车中进行了演示。
summary_zh: 基于学习的模型预测控制（MPC）能够通过修正模型误差来提升控制性能，其状态轨迹预测精度优于传统MPC方法。现有研究通常将未知残余动力学建模为高斯过程（GP），该方法既能利用数据优势，又可提供关联不确定性的量化估计。然而，在线学习的高计算成本成为实时GP-MPC应用面临的主要挑战。本研究提出一种近似时空高斯过程模型的高效实现方案，可在恒定计算复杂度下完成在线学习。该方案专为GP-MPC优化，通过实时在线学习更精确的系统动力学模型（即使在时变系统中），实现了控制性能的提升。通过自主微型赛车应用的仿真与硬件实验，验证了所提方法的性能表现。

title: FastLoop: Parallel Loop Closing with GPU-Acceleration in Visual SLAM
summary: Visual SLAM systems combine visual tracking with global loop closure to maintain a consistent map and accurate localization. Loop closure is a computationally expensive process as we need to search across the whole map for matches. This paper presents FastLoop, a GPU-accelerated loop closing module to alleviate this computational complexity. We identify key performance bottlenecks in the loop closing pipeline of visual SLAM and address them through parallel optimizations on the GPU. Specifically, we use task-level and data-level parallelism and integrate a GPU-accelerated pose graph optimization. Our implementation is built on top of ORB-SLAM3 and leverages CUDA for GPU programming. Experimental results show that FastLoop achieves an average speedup of 1.4x and 1.3x on the EuRoC dataset and 3.0x and 2.4x on the TUM-VI dataset for the loop closing module on desktop and embedded platforms, respectively, while maintaining the accuracy of the original system.
tldr: FastLoop introduces a GPU-accelerated loop closing module for Visual SLAM, achieving significant speedups on both desktop and embedded platforms while maintaining accuracy.
tldr_zh: FastLoop提出了一种用于视觉SLAM的GPU加速闭环模块，在保持精度的同时，在桌面和嵌入式平台上实现了显著的加速效果。
summary_zh: 视觉SLAM系统通过将视觉跟踪与全局闭环检测相结合，以维持地图一致性并实现精确定位。由于需要在整张地图中搜索匹配特征，闭环检测过程存在较高的计算开销。本文提出FastLoop——一种基于GPU加速的闭环检测模块以缓解该计算复杂度。我们分析了视觉SLAM闭环流程中的关键性能瓶颈，并通过GPU并行优化策略予以解决：采用任务级与数据级并行机制，并集成GPU加速的位姿图优化模块。该实现基于ORB-SLAM3框架，使用CUDA进行GPU编程。实验结果表明，在保持原系统精度的前提下，FastLoop在EuRoC数据集上使闭环模块在桌面端与嵌入式平台分别实现1.4倍与1.3倍加速，在TUM-VI数据集上则分别达到3.0倍与2.4倍加速效果。

title: SLAM Adversarial Lab: An Extensible Framework for Visual SLAM Robustness Evaluation under Adverse Conditions
summary: We present SAL (SLAM Adversarial Lab), a modular framework for evaluating visual SLAM systems under adversarial conditions such as fog and rain. SAL represents each adversarial condition as a perturbation that transforms an existing dataset into an adversarial dataset. When transforming a dataset, SAL supports severity levels using easily-interpretable real-world units such as meters for fog visibility. SAL's extensible architecture decouples datasets, perturbations, and SLAM algorithms through common interfaces, so users can add new components without rewriting integration code. Moreover, SAL includes a search procedure that finds the severity level of a perturbation at which a SLAM system fails. To showcase the capabilities of SAL, our evaluation integrates seven SLAM algorithms and evaluates them across three datasets under weather, camera, and video transport perturbations.
tldr: The paper introduces SAL, a modular framework for evaluating visual SLAM systems under adverse conditions like fog and rain, with extensible architecture and severity level support.
tldr_zh: 该论文介绍了SAL，一个模块化框架，用于评估视觉SLAM系统在雾和雨等不利条件下的性能，具有可扩展架构和可解释的严重程度支持。
summary_zh: 本文提出SAL（SLAM对抗实验室），一个模块化框架，用于评估视觉SLAM系统在雾、雨等对抗条件下的性能。SAL将每种对抗条件表示为一种扰动，可将现有数据集转化为对抗数据集。在转换数据集时，SAL支持采用易于理解的现实世界单位（如雾能见度的米数）来设定扰动强度等级。该框架通过通用接口实现数据集、扰动与SLAM算法的解耦，其可扩展架构使用户能添加新组件而无需重写集成代码。此外，SAL还包含搜索程序，可找出导致SLAM系统失效的临界扰动强度。为展示SAL的功能，我们集成7种SLAM算法进行评测，在三种数据集上测试了天气干扰、相机噪声和视频传输扰动等场景下的系统表现。
